message:
fix(search/vector_index): FtviWriter::finish now patches the count header

Fixes ft-yv02l. Previously `FtviWriter::finish` was a no-op — the
count-header field at bytes 8..12 stayed at the 0u32 placeholder
written in `FtviWriter::new`, so any attempt to round-trip the
writer's output through `FtviIndex::from_bytes` always yielded an
empty index (count==0, no records). The byte stream after position
12 contained the real records but the parser trusted the count
header and stopped there.

The trailing doc comment on the old `finish` acknowledged this:
"NOTE: The count header field requires a seekable writer to patch.
For non-seekable writers, use `finish_to_vec` instead." — but there
was no `finish_to_vec`, so callers writing into `&mut Vec<u8>` (or
any `Write`-only sink) silently produced broken indexes.

## What changed

- `FtviWriter<W>` now requires `W: Write + Seek` (was `W: Write`).
  The count-header offset is captured via `stream_position()` in
  `new` — which lets finish patch even when the writer did NOT start
  at offset 0 (e.g. appending into a pre-existing file or an FTVI
  region inside a larger container artifact).
- `finish(mut self)` seeks to the captured header offset, writes the
  final `self.count.to_le_bytes()`, then seeks back to end-of-stream
  and returns the writer. Downstream callers can keep appending.
- The streaming-writer doc comment is updated to steer callers with
  non-seekable sinks toward `write_ftvi_vec`, which is the existing
  one-shot path that builds the full bytes in memory.

## Call-site impact

Callers that passed `&mut Vec<u8>` (which is `Write` but not `Seek`)
no longer compile. They must wrap in `std::io::Cursor`:

    let mut buf = Vec::new();
    let mut w = FtviWriter::new(std::io::Cursor::new(&mut buf), dim)?;

`Vec<u8>` alone does not implement `Seek`; `Cursor<&mut Vec<u8>>`
does. The two test modules below are the full set of in-tree
callers and have been updated.

## Tests

- `tests/vector_index_tests.rs::ftvi_writer_tracks_count` is
  extended with a `FtviIndex::from_bytes(&buf)` round-trip that
  asserts the parsed index length matches the number of pushed
  records. This is the regression guard for ft-yv02l.
- `tests/proptest_vector_index.rs::writer_finish_produces_parseable_bytes`
  (property 31) is tightened: in addition to the previously-asserted
  magic/version/dimension fields, it now asserts the count header
  contains the record count and that `FtviIndex::from_bytes(&buf)`
  produces an index whose `.len()` equals the pushed-record count.
  The old comment noting "finish doesn't patch count" is removed
  now that it does.
- Writer-creation sites in both suites switched from
  `FtviWriter::new(&mut buf, dim)` to
  `FtviWriter::new(Cursor::new(&mut buf), dim)` to match the new
  `W: Write + Seek` bound.

## Scope notes

- Behavior on already-correct code paths (e.g. `write_ftvi_vec`,
  which builds the full byte buffer in one shot via
  `build_ftvi_bytes`) is unchanged. Only the streaming writer path
  now produces spec-conformant output.
- The FTVI on-disk format (magic "FTVI", version 1, u16 dimension,
  u32 count, f16 record payload) is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
